title: Prison Break In

jokes.text: What do you need? / Need? Why do you think I need something?
jokes.setup: Michael compliments his mother
jokes.punchline: She immediately assumes he wants something
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment for Lucille - cynical and transactional even with compliments. Sharp structure and economical delivery.

jokes.text: Or should I say... 'play dates.'
jokes.setup: Mentioning Michael's dates with Rita
jokes.punchline: Cruel implication about Rita's mental disability
jokes.explanation: Cruel but clever wordplay that's perfectly in character for Lucille. The pause before 'play dates' adds comedic weight.

jokes.text: Michael had been so devastated by their breakup that he'd even broken out the old guitar
jokes.setup: Michael claims he's relieved about the breakup
jokes.punchline: Narrator reveals he was so devastated he's playing guitar
jokes.explanation: Brilliant use of narrator to contradict character's claim. 'Broken out the old guitar' is perfect phrasing for sad-guy cliche.

jokes.text: I don't know, does she play guitar?
jokes.setup: Michael suggests talking to someone, like his mom
jokes.punchline: Lucille completely misses the therapy suggestion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille moment - completely oblivious to emotional subtext. Economical and character-specific.

jokes.text: And how about that little piece of tail on her? / I've opened a door here that I regret.
jokes.setup: Buster seems to be making sexual comment about his mother
jokes.punchline: Michael's horrified reaction, then revelation it's about a turtle
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with perfect character reaction. 'I've opened a door here that I regret' is quotable gold.

jokes.text: She has a name, Mother. And it's 'Mother.'
jokes.setup: Buster defending his turtle
jokes.punchline: The turtle is named after his mother
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that reveals Buster's psychological issues. Perfect economy and character integration. Quintessential Arrested Development.

jokes.text: Stay in this box that Uncle/Father Oscar left behind. In fact, it was a box of Oscar's legally obtained medical marijuana.
jokes.setup: Buster telling turtle to stay in Oscar's box
jokes.punchline: Narrator reveals it's full of marijuana
jokes.explanation: Good visual setup for upcoming marijuana wordplay. 'Legally obtained' is nice sarcastic touch.

jokes.text: Oh, good, you've already got grass. / Primo bud. Real sticky weed.
jokes.setup: Lucille sees the turtle setup
jokes.punchline: Both unknowingly using marijuana terminology about lawn grass
jokes.explanation: Brilliant double entendre with both characters oblivious. Multiple layers of wordplay executed perfectly.

jokes.text: TBA debacle flashback sequence
jokes.setup: Family trying to choose a disease for charity
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous suggestions escalating to sending invitations for 'TBA'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist premise that escalates perfectly. The family's dysfunction creating accidental charity success.

jokes.text: Somebody saw Seinfeld last night. / Ovarian cancer. Gee, I wonder who that was.
jokes.setup: Family suggesting diseases
jokes.punchline: Meta joke about TV influence and obvious personal connection
jokes.explanation: Good character moments with meta-commentary. Lucille's sarcasm about Lindsay's obvious suggestion.

jokes.text: Much to their surprise, the Bluths ended up raising over $25,000 to fight 'TBA.'
jokes.setup: The TBA mistake
jokes.punchline: People actually donated to fight a non-existent disease
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the absurd premise. The narrator's matter-of-fact delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: And then—and here's the really horrible part—they did it again the following year.
jokes.setup: The original TBA scam
jokes.punchline: They repeated the same scam the next year
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation showing the family's shamelessness. Narrator's moral judgment adds to the humor.

jokes.text: Keep fighting, little guy. With your support of the Bluth Foundation, we'll live in a galaxy where 'TBA' does not exist.
jokes.setup: The ongoing TBA deception
jokes.punchline: Grandiose speech about eliminating a non-disease from the galaxy
jokes.explanation: Escalates the absurdity with George Sr.'s over-the-top rhetoric. 'Galaxy' is perfect overreach.

jokes.text: His hobby was making papier-mâché copies of his own head.
jokes.setup: George Sr. claims he has a wonderful hobby
jokes.punchline: Narrator reveals the bizarre and narcissistic nature of the hobby
jokes.explanation: Hilariously absurd hobby that's perfectly in character. The visual of multiple George Sr. heads is comedy gold.

jokes.text: I promised myself I wasn't going to become one of those people who were always going on about their screenplays.
jokes.setup: Warden mentions his screenplay
jokes.punchline: Immediately becomes exactly the person he doesn't want to be
jokes.explanation: Classic self-aware irony. The character does exactly what he claims he won't do.

jokes.text: Warden Gentles had convinced his granddaughters' teacher to mount New Warden as a school play.
jokes.setup: Warden talking about his successful staged reading
jokes.punchline: It was actually just a school play with children
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deflation of the warden's artistic pretensions. The reveal recontextualizes everything.

jokes.text: Nobody sells any coke in this pen without daddy getting a taste. / Beat him with a pillow case full of batteries.
jokes.setup: Children performing the warden's prison drama
jokes.punchline: Kids delivering hardcore prison dialogue
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe comedy - innocent children saying violent prison dialogue. The juxtaposition is brilliantly uncomfortable.

jokes.text: Oh, right. Uh, he spoke at my school for a 'Startled Straight' assembly.
jokes.setup: Lindsay explaining how she knows the warden
jokes.punchline: Malapropism of 'Scared Straight' program
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lindsay malapropism showing her disconnect from reality. 'Startled' is a great substitute.

jokes.text: I think you will find the dessert both engrossing and high grossing. / So, we don't get dessert?
jokes.setup: Lindsay pitching her film project
jokes.punchline: Warden thinks 'dessert' refers to actual food
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection wordplay. Shows both characters' confusion in different ways.

jokes.text: G.V.H.: Graft Versus Host. It's new, it's fatal, and guess who's got it?
jokes.setup: Lucille found a real disease for the charity
jokes.punchline: Her own husband has the deadly disease she's using for fundraising
jokes.explanation: Dark but brilliant - using your husband's illness for charity with casual delivery. Perfect Lucille moment.

jokes.text: Either you remove your hair graft, or the host—you—will get sicker and eventually die.
jokes.setup: Explaining Tobias's condition
jokes.punchline: Hair transplant is literally killing him
jokes.explanation: Absurd medical condition that's both ridiculous and genuinely threatening. Perfect setup for vanity vs. survival.

jokes.text: Can you think of any benefits? / Benefits? No. He'll die.
jokes.setup: Lucille asking about benefits of the disease
jokes.punchline: Doctor's blunt response about death
jokes.explanation: Dark humor from Lucille's mercenary charity planning. Doctor's deadpan response is perfect.

jokes.text: Oh, yeah, no, I get that. But have there ever been any fund-raisers?
jokes.setup: Doctor says Tobias will die
jokes.punchline: Lucille only cares about fundraising potential
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille - completely callous about family member's death, focused on social opportunities.

jokes.text: Did you see the attention Superman's wife got?
jokes.setup: Justifying exploiting Tobias's disease
jokes.punchline: Reference to real-world tragedy as social template
jokes.explanation: Dark reference to real tragedy shows Lucille's warped priorities. Slightly cheap but in character.

jokes.text: Oh! Oh! This is going hurt! / You know what? We're going to need this from another angle.
jokes.setup: Tobias in pain during video shoot
jokes.punchline: Gob wants to film his suffering from multiple angles
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing the family's callousness toward Tobias's real suffering.

jokes.text: Who's the 'her' in that sentence? / Mom. Warden Gentles likes Mom. / 'Her?'
jokes.setup: Lindsay suggesting the warden likes Lucille
jokes.punchline: Michael's repeated disbelief
jokes.explanation: Good reaction comedy showing Michael's inability to process his mother as romantic figure.

jokes.text: The guy runs a prison, he can have any piece of ass he wants.
jokes.setup: Discussion of the warden's options
jokes.punchline: Gob's crude assessment of prison dating advantages
jokes.explanation: Typical Gob crudeness but not particularly clever. Character-appropriate but low-effort humor.

jokes.text: Oh, come on, don't leave your Uncle Teabag hanging. / Please don't call yourself that.
jokes.setup: Tobias trying to connect with George Michael
jokes.punchline: Accidentally using sexual slang as nickname
jokes.explanation: Classic Tobias double entendre. His obliviousness to sexual connotations is perfect character comedy.

jokes.text: What he was having trouble with was looking directly at his dying uncle.
jokes.setup: George Michael seeming uncomfortable
jokes.punchline: It's not the nickname but Tobias's deteriorating condition
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection revealing the real source of discomfort. Narrator's matter-of-fact tone helps.

jokes.text: Uncle/Father Oscar? What are you doing here?
jokes.setup: Oscar's arrival
jokes.punchline: Buster's confused family terminology
jokes.explanation: Great running gag callback. The slashed relationship terms perfectly capture Buster's confusion.

jokes.text: I could never leave a little bud behind.
jokes.setup: Oscar explaining his return
jokes.punchline: Double meaning of marijuana bud and Buster
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay connecting the marijuana plot with paternal affection. Perfect economy.

jokes.text: Oh, yeah. My turtle was living in it before he ate all the grass and died.
jokes.setup: Oscar looking for his marijuana box
jokes.punchline: Turtle died from eating the marijuana
jokes.explanation: Dark payoff to earlier setup. The casual delivery of pet death from drug consumption.

jokes.text: At least you can stay and help me bury my poor turtle. / Or we could cremate it.
jokes.setup: Buster wants to bury the turtle
jokes.punchline: Oscar suggests cremation (smoking the marijuana-filled turtle)
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark wordplay. Oscar wants to smoke the marijuana-filled turtle corpse. Perfect character motivation.

jokes.text: You can't park there! No stair car parking! / Sorry, I forgot.
jokes.setup: Michael arriving at the prison
jokes.punchline: Specific signage against the family's distinctive vehicle
jokes.explanation: Great running gag payoff. The prison specifically banning their vehicle shows ongoing problems.

jokes.text: I've had to go to his parole hearings twice, and lie through my teeth to keep him here.
jokes.setup: Praising the cook
jokes.punchline: He prevents the cook's release for selfish culinary reasons
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor showing the warden's corruption. Casual admission of keeping someone imprisoned for personal benefit.

jokes.text: I might have you beaten with a pillowcase full of batteries.
jokes.setup: Warden explaining his perspective on Michael's visit
jokes.punchline: Casually threatening violence from his screenplay
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the children's performance. Shows the warden's casual relationship with violence.

jokes.text: Who's the 'I' in that sentence? / Me. / You? / Her.
jokes.setup: Lucille reveals she's trying to seduce the warden
jokes.punchline: Michael's confusion about pronouns showing his disbelief
jokes.explanation: Great economy showing Michael's complete inability to process his mother's sexuality. Perfect pronoun confusion.

jokes.text: Plus, she's married, okay? She can't be writhing around underneath... What is wrong with you?! It's disgusting! God!
jokes.setup: Michael explaining why mother can't have affair
jokes.punchline: Gets graphically sexual and disgusts himself
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Michael's psychological issues with parents

jokes.text: But, thankfully, he'd found a way to forget that. / I've got a thing of pills in my pocket.
jokes.setup: Narrator mentioning Gob witnessed parents' intimacy
jokes.punchline: Gob immediately takes forget-me-now pills
jokes.explanation: Great callback showing Gob's coping mechanism. The timing of the pill-taking is perfect.

jokes.text: Listen, new fish, that bunk was open because the last guy wouldn't do the things that you're going to do. / We're going to do things?
jokes.setup: George Michael reading what he thinks is Maeby's script message to him
jokes.punchline: His innocent interpretation of prison dialogue
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy from George Michael misinterpreting prison script as romantic message from cousin.

jokes.text: Don't make the water too hot—the scabs come right off.
jokes.setup: Instructing George Michael to help Tobias bathe
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of Tobias's deteriorating condition
jokes.explanation: Good gross-out humor delivered casually. Shows the family's normalization of Tobias's condition.

jokes.text: Oh, it's on 'standby'! / Got another one in you?
jokes.setup: Buster trying to hide Oscar in the cage
jokes.punchline: Oscar gets knocked out by the cage lid again
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy callback. The repetitive nature of Oscar's head injuries is the joke.

jokes.text: Although, it wasn't the first time he'd been knocked out by a powerful lid.
jokes.setup: Oscar gets knocked out by something
jokes.punchline: Reference to being knocked out by LSD ('lid' is slang for LSD)
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that works on multiple levels and fits Oscar's character

jokes.text: Oh, then why do we care? / What is wrong with everyone?
jokes.setup: Buster learning it's not another brother with their mother
jokes.punchline: He loses all interest, Michael's exasperation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Buster's warped priorities and Michael's frustration with family dysfunction.

jokes.text: Well... where the hell else are they gonna go at 4:00 in the afternoon?
jokes.setup: Michael asking how George Sr. knows about the office meeting
jokes.punchline: George Sr. revealing he's been monitoring everything
jokes.explanation: Good revelation moment showing George Sr.'s awareness of the situation. Sets up the escape plot reveal.

jokes.text: Howdy doody, that's it.
jokes.setup: George Sr. getting a call with code phrase
jokes.punchline: Using children's TV show reference as escape code
jokes.explanation: Absurd but character-appropriate code phrase. The innocent reference for criminal activity is funny.

jokes.text: She just called to ask me to bring her a tube of vag... ...table paste. Hi, George Michael.
jokes.setup: Lindsay about to reveal intimate detail
jokes.punchline: Awkward save when George Michael appears
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with awkward timing. The 'vegetable paste' save is transparently bad.

jokes.text: But this, too, would prove challenging, as the family had no books. Other than those.
jokes.setup: Michael wanting to read instead of going to the gala
jokes.punchline: Family only has pornographic magazines
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag setup and payoff. Shows the family's lack of intellectual material.

jokes.text: Enjoy the chlamydia, Lucille. / Take this newly-diseased woman to the hole.
jokes.setup: Warden's screenplay revenge fantasy
jokes.punchline: Graphic details of sexually transmitted disease revenge
jokes.explanation: Dark revenge fantasy that escalates appropriately. Shows the warden's true vindictive nature.

jokes.text: I wish I'd read The Man Inside Me.
jokes.setup: Michael realizing he should have read Tobias's book
jokes.punchline: Reference to Tobias's accidentally sexual self-help title
jokes.explanation: Good callback to running gag. Michael's earnest wish makes the sexual connotation funnier.

jokes.text: It's not a Jamba Juice, Michael. / Shouldn't be that hard to break into.
jokes.setup: Gob saying prison isn't easy to break into
jokes.punchline: Michael's assumption that Jamba Juice would be harder
jokes.explanation: Good unexpected comparison. The implication that Jamba Juice has better security than prison.

jokes.text: Wait a minute. You're not really smiling. / I'm Oscar.
jokes.setup: Buster examining who he thinks is his father
jokes.punchline: Realizes it's actually Oscar based on facial expression
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the twin switching plot. Buster's method of detection is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: It's a jetpack, Michael. What could possibly go wrong?
jokes.setup: Michael questioning the jetpack plan
jokes.punchline: Classic tempting-fate line before disaster
jokes.explanation: Classic setup for disaster. Predictable but character-appropriate for Gob's overconfidence.

jokes.text: Good. Now we know what the strap was for.
jokes.setup: Jetpack disaster
jokes.punchline: Michael's dry observation about protective equipment
jokes.explanation: Good dry reaction to physical comedy. Michael's understated response to chaos.

jokes.text: Drew a map of it on my stomach. It's upside down, so it might be a little awkward. / It was awkward.
jokes.setup: Gob's prison map solution
jokes.punchline: Narrator confirming the obvious awkwardness
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy setup with narrator's dry confirmation. The awkwardness is both visual and social.

jokes.text: If you're gonna party, you want to bring it inside? That was a freebie.
jokes.setup: Guard catches Michael and Gob with jetpack
jokes.punchline: Treats it casually and offers joke for free
jokes.explanation: Mild humor from guard's casual attitude toward obvious escape attempt

jokes.text: That would be the happiest moment George Michael would ever experience in his life.
jokes.setup: George Michael realizing the script isn't about him and Maeby
jokes.punchline: Narrator's over-the-top assessment of this minor relief
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor about George Michael's low expectations for happiness. Perfect narrator escalation.

jokes.text: I don't think there's going to be an 'I'm Oscar' website this time. I think this time I'll do a podcast.
jokes.setup: Warden having George Sr. back in custody
jokes.punchline: Reference to previous escape method, updated to current technology
jokes.explanation: Good callback updated for contemporary technology. Shows the warden's adaptation to previous tricks.

jokes.text: Sounds like a tennis match between Steffi Graf and Happy Days star Donny Host. / It's Most.
jokes.setup: Gob's description of Graft versus Host
jokes.punchline: Wrong actor name, narrator's correction
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay with typical Gob mistake. Not particularly strong but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: No, not that candy ball machine.
jokes.setup: Gob talking about things he can't make disappear
jokes.punchline: Specifically excludes a candy machine, implying he can make that disappear
jokes.explanation: Mild visual gag showing Gob's actual magical capabilities

jokes.text: When Toby first came into the family, none of us much wanted him around. But that's because we couldn't see what was on the inside. But lately, that's all we've been able to see.
jokes.setup: Gob's charity video about accepting Tobias
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation due to Tobias's deteriorating condition
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark wordplay turning inspirational message into grotesque reality. Perfect structure and payoff.

jokes.text: No way. That horse hair is my ticket back into society.
jokes.setup: Learning he could be cured by removing the hair
jokes.punchline: Prioritizing vanity over survival
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tobias moment - choosing appearance over health. Character-driven comedy at its best.

jokes.text: I'd rather give to T.B.A.! This is bull(bleep)!
jokes.setup: Crowd learning Tobias just needs to remove his hair
jokes.punchline: Preferring the fake disease to this real situation
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback escalation. The fake disease is preferable to this real but stupid situation.

jokes.text: Just as the prisoners had proven they could throw a gala as well as the rich, so the rich proved they could throw a riot just as well as the prisoners.
jokes.setup: Chaos breaking out at the charity gala
jokes.punchline: Comparing rich people's riot behavior to prisoners
jokes.explanation: Brilliant social commentary with perfect parallel structure. Shows the thin veneer of civilization.

jokes.text: I want to cry so bad. But I don't think I can spare the moisture.
jokes.setup: Lucille having an emotional moment with Michael
jokes.punchline: Even in vulnerability, maintaining her vanity priorities
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille moment - vulnerability immediately undercut by vanity. Quotable gold with perfect character consistency.

jokes.text: You gave him chlamydia? / He had crabs, and I said, 'You're gonna get laughs.'
jokes.setup: Michael thinking Lucille gave the warden an STD
jokes.punchline: She was giving comedy writing advice about his script
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection with perfect wordplay. 'Crabs' works on multiple levels - STD and comedy term.

jokes.text: I can't say no to the woman who gave me chlamydia.
jokes.setup: Lucille asking for the deal
jokes.punchline: Warden's romantic interpretation of the comedy advice
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the misunderstanding. The warden's romantic interpretation of script advice.

jokes.text: That I didn't need to hear.
jokes.setup: Parents about to be intimate
jokes.punchline: Michael's horrified reaction to overhearing
jokes.explanation: Good cringe reaction. Michael's ongoing discomfort with his parents' sexuality.

jokes.text: We ready to have some fun? I'm so glad I didn't cry. That I didn't need to hear.
jokes.setup: Parents about to have intimate time
jokes.punchline: Michael forced to hear inappropriate details
jokes.explanation: Standard cringe humor from child overhearing parents' sexual plans

jokes.text: George Michael experiences his unhappiest moment ever.
jokes.setup: George Michael realizing he's sharing a cell with male cousin
jokes.punchline: Narrator's dramatic contrast to his earlier 'happiest moment'
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback with escalation. The extreme emotional swing from relief to horror is brilliantly structured.

jokes.text: No! Not here! Wait. I've seen this before. Oh, God, I've seen this before!
jokes.setup: Gob facing his parents being intimate again
jokes.punchline: His horror at the recurring traumatic experience
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Gob's recurring trauma. The repetitive nature of his horror is well-executed.

tmdb_overview: The family plans their annual fund raiser, and they actually have a cause this time. Tobias is ailing due to his transplanted hair plugs, which appear to be rejecting him. Lindsay uses the illness as her ticket back into high society, and Michael learns of the prison warden's true intentions with his mother after reading the warden's screenplay.
insight: Prison subplot hits 86 with 2.56 jokes per minute—peak Arrested chaos density.
